html:
Exclusive to:

Nettle Rolls are a Crock Pot dish introduced in the Hamlet DLC. They require at least 3 Nettles to cook, and a filler, which cannot be a Mandrake. Adding Vegetables as filler may result in Ratatouille, while adding Fruits as filler may result in Fist Full of Jam.

Nettle Rolls temporarily alleviate Hay Fever without the need of a Gas Mask for 720 seconds (or one and a half game-days). When eaten, they restore 20 Health, 25 Hunger and 5 Sanity.

Prerequisites

  • Requires :
  • Excludes :

Recipe

×3

Examples

×3

×3

50% Chance of cooking Ratatouille

×3

50% Chance of cooking Fist Full of Jam

Tips

  • Wigfrid is unable to eat Nettles or Nettle Rolls, so the player will need to rely on Meated Nettles or have a Gas Mask prepared before the Lush Season begins to avoid Hay Fever.
  • The player will need to amass 24 raw Nettles to sustain each Lush Season.
  • The player should keep in mind that Nettle Rolls alleviates Hay Fever for 120 seconds longer than its raw counterpart (3 raw Nettles alleviate hay fever for 600 seconds total) and restores a tiny bit of . However, Nettle Rolls spoil, heal 10 less, and fills 5 less than its 3 raw counterparts.
    • Alternatively, the player can use the insulated pack's ability to slow food spoilage to carry around all the Nettle Rolls they will need for the season around with them. Assuming the player cooks them all at the start of the season, only two or three will become stale before the hay fever ends. The player will need seven or eight Nettle Rolls to get through the season, depending on how much they plan to utilize the Gas Mask.

Trivia

  • During the Pig Fiesta event, Nettle Rolls take on a more festive appearance.

'''Nettle Rolls''' are a [[Crock Pot]] dish introduced in the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC. They require at least 3 [[Nettle]]s to cook, and a filler, which cannot be a [[Mandrake]]. Adding [[Vegetable]]s as filler may result in [[Ratatouille]], while adding [[Fruit]]s as filler may result in [[Fist Full of Jam]].

Nettle Rolls temporarily alleviate [[Hay Fever]] without the need of a [[Gas Mask]] for 720 seconds (or one and a half [[Day-Night Cycle|game-days]]). When eaten, they restore 20 Health, 25 Hunger and 5 Sanity.

== Prerequisites ==
* Requires : {{pic|64|Nettle}}
* Excludes : {{pic|64|Mandrake}}

== {{pic|32|Cookbook}} Recipe ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Nettle
|count1 = 3
|item2 = Filler
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Nettle Rolls
}}

== Examples ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Nettle
|count1 = 3
|item2 = Twigs
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Nettle Rolls
}}
{{Recipe
|item1 = Nettle
|count1 = 3
|item2 = Radishes
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Nettle Rolls
|note = 50% Chance of cooking Ratatouille
}}
{{Recipe
|item1 = Nettle
|count1 = 3
|item2 = Berry
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Nettle Rolls
|note = 50% Chance of cooking Fist Full of Jam
}}

== {{pic|32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* [[Wigfrid]] is unable to eat Nettles or Nettle Rolls, so the player will need to rely on <u>[[Meated Nettles]]</u> or have a [[Gas Mask]] prepared before the [[Lush Season]] begins to avoid Hay Fever.
* The player will need to amass 24 raw [[Nettle]]s to sustain each [[Lush Season]].
* The player should keep in mind that Nettle Rolls alleviates Hay Fever for 120 seconds longer than its raw counterpart (3 raw Nettles alleviate hay fever for 600 seconds total) and restores a tiny bit of {{sanity}}. However, Nettle Rolls spoil, heal 10 {{hp}} less, and fills 5 less {{hunger}} than its 3 raw counterparts.
**Alternatively, the player can use the insulated pack's ability to slow food spoilage to carry around all the Nettle Rolls they will need for the season around with them. Assuming the player cooks them all at the start of the season, only two or three will become stale before the hay fever ends. The player will need seven or eight Nettle Rolls to get through the season, depending on how much they plan to utilize the Gas Mask.

== {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
[[File:Nettle Rolls (Pig Fiesta).png|right]]
* During the [[Pig Fiesta]] event, Nettle Rolls take on a more festive appearance.
